The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Passeroidea; Fringillidae; Carduelinae; Chloris
Assembly: This project presents the diploid genome assembly of the European greenfinch, Chloris Chloris, from a sample provided by Jan T. Lifjeld. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The bChlchl1.1 assembly is based on 29x PacBio HiFi and 76x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been included in hap1.

Assembly: The assembly bLarArg3.hap1.1 is based on 75x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Assembly: The assembly bChrRid1.1 is based on 37x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Assembly: The assembly bCapEur3.1 is based on 87x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Arrow polishing assisted by Merfin, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
